title: TDX Realtime Snapshot Wire (schemaVersion=0, draftRevision=1)
description: Language-agnostic contract for the experimental TDX realtime snapshot pushed from datasource to Mist over the experimental WebSocket. Both the Python datasource decoder and the TypeScript Mist client MUST conform to this schema. This is the single source of truth — TypeScript interfaces and Pydantic models are generated/derived from this, not the other way around.

payloadType.description: Stable semantic name. Experimental status is expressed via contractStatus, not the payloadType name. Future v1 upgrade does not rename this.

schemaVersion.description: Wire schema version. Increment only when the typed contract changes (field names, types, required-ness, units, time, epoch/sequence semantics). Adding a native alias to the decoder without changing the typed wire does NOT increment this.

draftRevision.description: Draft revision within schemaVersion=0. The datasource emits exactly one revision; the Mist build accepts exactly one precise tuple (payloadType, schemaVersion, draftRevision, acquisitionProfile). Mismatch does not enter subscription state — record stable error + metric, no degradation, no conversion, no simultaneous multi-revision.

acquisitionProfile.description: The SDK method used to acquire this snapshot. Declared, not verified — Windows HIL ultimately confirms. Do not write as verified fact.

streamEpoch.description: Opaque non-empty string identifying the current stream generation. Generated by the datasource gateway when an owner generation changes. The Mist store fences on (instrumentKey, streamEpoch, sequence). Snapshots MUST NOT implicitly switch epoch — only stream_started/ready events do.

sequence.description: Authoritative monotonic outbound sequence assigned by the gateway (not the terminal's producerSequence). Monotonic per (instrumentKey, streamEpoch). Duplicate or out-of-order sequences are dropped before any await publisher. 1..Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER.

symbol.description: Provider transport code with exchange suffix, e.g. '600519.SH'. Must match an exact allowlist entry (case-sensitive). NOT a canonical bare code.

capturedAt.description: RFC3339 with offset. The wall-clock time at which the terminal bridge completed the native get_market_snapshot call. Set by the terminal worker, not the gateway.

snapshot.description: Typed price fields extracted from native projection. last is required and finite. Others are always present; missing native values are null (NOT 0).

last.description: Current/last price. Required, finite (reject NaN, Infinity, -Infinity, booleans).

nativeVolume.description: Native cumulative volume, unit unverified (may be lots or shares depending on TDX build). Marked native/unverified until HIL.

nativeAmount.description: Native cumulative amount, unit unverified (may be CNY or 10000-CNY). Marked native/unverified until HIL.

unitStatus.description: Volume/amount units are native and unverified until Windows HIL confirms.

quality.description: Quality markers. Empty object means clean. Presence of a key means degraded.

eventTime.description: RFC3339 with offset, or null. Native exchange time if the provider supplies a trustworthy one. If missing, null (never synthesized from clock). quality.nativeTimeUnavailable=true in that case.
